rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION: $129,000.00 U.S. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Set Aside Stay And Set Case For Priority Civil Jury Trial
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 24, 2018, Line 1, RESPONDENT EVA SCALLA's Motion To Set Aside Stay And Set Case For Priority Civil Jury Trial Claimant Eva Scalla's motion to set aside stay is treated as a motion to lift the stay effected by the stipulated order filed April 28, 2017 and, as so treated, it is granted due to no opposition by the petitioner People of the State of California. To the extent the motion seeks to vacate the order filed April 28, 2017, that portion of the motion is denied because there is no basis to vacate that order. The portion of the motion requesting that the case be set for a jury trial on a priority basis is taken off calendar without prejudice to being refiled to be heard in department 206, which is the department that hears motions to set trials on a priority basis. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Scalla is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: KAMAILEIMILA DEL FIERRO VS. DESOTO CAB COMPANY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Desoto Cab Company, Inc.'S Motion To Dismiss; And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 24, 2018, Line 13, Defendant Desoto Cab Company, Inc.'S Motion To Dismiss; And For Sanctions Defendant Desoto Cab Company, Inc.'s motion to dismiss and for sanctions is denied in its entirety. Assuming without deciding that plaintiff Kamaileimila Del Ferro has failed to pay a monetary sanctions order, that failure is not a basis to dismiss this case in the face of clear evidence that plaintiff wishes to proceed with the case. The remedy for an unpaid monetary sanctions order is to convert it into a judgment or use it as a credit against any monies awarded to the sanctioned party at the conclusion of the case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: MERRILEE HARRIS VS. COLINDRES & ASSOCIATES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Collindres & Associates' Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 24, 2018, Line 4, Defendant Collindres & Associates' Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Cross-Complaint Cross-complainant Colindres & Associates, Inc.'s motion for leave to file a second amended cross-complaint is denied. While California has a liberal policy favoring allowing amendments to pleadings, that policy is insufficient to warrant the granting of this motion due to the undue prejudice that would be suffered by plaintiff Merrilee Harris if the motion is granted. Permitting the filing of the second amended cross-complaint would inevitably result in a lengthy continuance of the trial and would require Ms. Harris to participate in greatly increased discovery proceedings. C&A is not prejudiced by the denial of this motion since its indemnity claims can be alleged in a separate lawsuit and are not even ripe unless and until there is a judgment against C&A. C&A's argument that the recent additions to Ms. Harris's complaint requires that C&A be able to cross-claim against a host of subcontractors is unpersuasive. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Harris is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: ENEIDA ANGELICA RECINOS VS. JOHN SMITH ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 24, 2018, Line 7, PLAINTIFF ENEIDA RECINOS' DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT Plaintiff Eneida Recinos' demurrer to the first through twelfth affirmative defenses in the answer filed by defendant John Smith is overruled as to all twelve affirmative defenses. Mr. Smith's pleading of his affirmative defenses comports with the longstanding practice for pleading affirmative defenses in Bay Area state courts based on the well-understood premise that at the outset of a case a defendant may know the reasonable range of possible affirmative defenses but may not yet have had the opportunity to determine whether there is factual support for them. If, after a reasonable opportunity for discovery and factual investigation has expired without Mr. Smith having any factual support for some or all of this affirmative defenses, counsel for Ms. Recinos should request that counsel for Mr. Smith withdraw those affirmative defenses and, if counsel for Ms. Recinos is unsatisfied by the response, then Ms. Recinos can bring a summary adjudication motion seeking to eliminate the affirmative defenses lacking any factual support. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Smith is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: ENEIDA ANGELICA RECINOS VS. JOHN SMITH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 24, 2018, Line 7, PLAINTIFF ENEIDA RECINOS' MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS Plaintiff Eneida Recinos' motion for judgment on the pleadings on the first, fourth, seventh through tenth and twelfth affirmative defenses in the answer filed by defendant RJR Transportation, Inc. is denied as to all seven affirmative defenses. RJR's pleading of its affirmative defenses comports with the longstanding practice for pleading affirmative defenses in Bay Area state courts based on the well-understood premise that at the outset of a case a defendant may know the reasonable range of possible affirmative defenses but may not yet have had the opportunity to determine whether there is factual support for them. If, after a reasonable opportunity for discovery and factual investigation has expired without RJR having any factual support for some or all of this affirmative defenses, counsel for Ms. Recinos should request that counsel for RJR withdraw those affirmative defenses and, if counsel for Ms. Recinos is unsatisfied by the response, then Ms. Recinos can bring a summary adjudication motion seeking to eliminate the affirmative defenses lacking any factual support. Insufficient time has elapsed for RJR to have a reasonable opportunity for discovery and factual investigation regarding its affirmative defenses. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for RJR is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: MIDLAND FUNDING LLC VS. NICHOLE CORNEJO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Establishing Admissions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 24, 2018, Line 3 PLAINTIFF MIDLAND FUNDING LLC'S Motion For Order Establishing Admissions Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff Midland Funding LLC's unopposed Motion for Order Establishing Admissions is GRANTED in full. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. See CCP § 2023.040. Plaintiff shall prepare a form of order attaching a copy of Requests for Admissions, Set One, as an exhibit to the order. Plaintiff may email a copy of the proposed order to the Judge Pro Tem before 5pm today. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
